humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates college-level expertise across arts and humanities disciplines (domain) by leveraging multimodal items consisting of historical cartoons, sheet music, and architectural floor plans (content).
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Combines text and images in a standard multiple-choice format, and also features a 'vision-only' setting where textual questions and choices are embedded as screenshots/photos to enforce simultaneous seeing and reading.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Factual and style recognition of artworks, music theory analysis from musical notation, historical interpretation of political cartoons, and complex visual-textual spatial reasoning.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates 30 academic subjects spanning Science, Tech, Health, Humanities, and Art & Design.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates state-of-the-art MLLMs including GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemini 1.5 Pro, LLaVA-OneVision, and InternVL2.

llm_is_primary_system.note: The primary objective is benchmarking the robust reasoning and multimodal understanding of MLLMs.

arts_humanities_data.note: Includes substantial evaluation data in Visual Arts (Art, Art Theory, Design), Music, History, and Literature.

quantitative_metrics.note: Utilizes classification accuracy for the multiple-choice tasks and Levenshtein distance for OCR similarity.

published_after_march_2023.note: The paper was first preprinted on arXiv in September 2024.

english_language.note: The paper and all benchmark questions are in English.

summary: MMMU-Pro introduces a more robust evaluation framework for the Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding benchmark to prevent shortcut exploitation by MLLMs. It eliminates text-only solvable questions, scales the candidate option space from 4 to 10 choices, and introduces a vision-only setting where questions are embedded directly into images. This design forces models to integrate visual and textual modalities holistically, exposing significant performance gaps in current frontier models.
key_findings: All evaluated MLLMs showed significant performance drops on MMMU-Pro compared to MMMU, with accuracy declines ranging from 16.8% to 26.9%. GPT-4o achieved the highest overall score with 54.0% in the standard 10-option setting and 49.7% in the vision-only setting. Chain of Thought (CoT) prompting showed strong gains in structured reasoning fields (e.g., Engineering) but was ineffective or even detrimental in subjective domains like Art and Design.
